items.title: Trump says war in Iran could be over soon, but U.S. hasn't 'won enough' yet
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump hinted on Monday that the war in the Middle East could be over soon — though not this week, he specified — even as hardliners pledged loyalty to their new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei.

items.title: TikTok allowed to keep business in Canada under new rules
items.summary_auto: The federal government said on Monday that it will let TikTok continue to operate in Canada and allow an investment by the tech ‌platform to proceed, after a national security review reversed the conclusion of a previous one.

items.title: FIFA World Cup COO says tournament 'too big' to be postponed amid global turmoil
items.summary_auto: FIFA's World Cup chief operating officer says the tournament is "too big" to be postponed because of global turmoil caused by the U.S. and Israeli war against Iran.

items.title: The National | Trump says U.S. hasn’t ‘won enough’ in Iran
items.summary_auto: March 9, 2026 | Trump says the war in Iran has been a success, but the U.S. hasn’t ‘won enough’ to end the attacks. A harrowing rescue in northern Ontario after an ice fishing group gets stranded on thin ice. And, a CBC News investigation finds Canadian armed forces members on a white supremacist dating site.

items.title: Family of Tumbler Ridge shooting victim suing OpenAI
items.summary_auto: The mother of Maya Gebala, a 12-year-old who remains in hospital after the shooting on Feb. 10, alleges the tech company failed to alert authorities to prompts from the shooter related to violence.

items.title: Estrogen patch shortage leaves some Albertans in a lurch, looking for alternatives
items.summary_auto: The latest wave of an ongoing shortage of estrogen patches has some Alberta women scrambling to find new menopause treatments.

items.title: Lawyer defends $30M settlement offer for victims of 'Mount Cashel 6' at B.C. boys high schools
items.summary_auto: "We can do some real good right now for survivors, and that's what's driving this settlement at this time," said Joe Fiorante, lawyer for the plaintiffs, in response to the settlement for students who suffered sexual, physical and psychological abuse.

items.title: Canada is off to a strong start at the Paralympics
items.summary_auto: CBC Sports' daily newsletter recaps the opening weekend of the Milan-Cortina Paralympic Winter Games and looks at the Canadians to watch on Tuesday.

items.title: Is 'oil rain' really falling in Iran?  | About That
items.summary_auto: Andrew Chang explains how burning oil from depots hit by U.S.-Israeli airstrikes is falling as black rain over parts of Iran, and the danger it poses. Images provided by The Canadian Press, Reuters and Getty Images

items.title: Middle East war casts uncertainty over 2026 FIFA World Cup
items.summary_auto: Growing conflict in the Middle East is creating uncertainty around the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with Iran scheduled to play matches in the United States despite the two countries now being involved in military strikes. Experts say the situation is unprecedented for FIFA, while members of Metro Vancouver's Iranian community say events back home have overshadowed the tournament.

items.title: What do you get when you win the U.K. Wife Carrying Race? Bragging rights and a barrel of ale
items.summary_auto: Teemu Touvinen sprinted over the hillside with his girlfriend Jatta Leinonen flung over his shoulder on Sunday, winning first place at the 2026 U.K. Wife Carrying Race in Dorking, England.

items.title: 'That ain't good': Ont. fisherman recounts harrowing helicopter rescue from drifting ice
items.summary_auto: As Ontario fisherman Kevin Fox filmed himself running across the ice in Georgian Bay near Owen Sound, Ont., Sunday afternoon, he realized the severity of his situation as it became clear that he and several others could no longer get back to shore.

items.title: 'That ain't good': Ont. fisherman recounts harrowing helicopter rescue from drifting ice on Georgian Bay
items.summary_auto: As Ontario fisherman Kevin Fox filmed himself running across the ice in Georgian Bay near Owen Sound, Ont., Sunday afternoon, he realized the severity of his situation as it became clear that he and several others could no longer get back to shore.

items.title: Fact check: Does this video show Tehran or L.A. fires?
items.summary_auto: CBC's fact check team geolocated this video to Tehran, but Elon Musk's AI chatbot Grok is telling X users it's a manipulated video of Los Angeles.

items.title: Bloc leader laughs off running 'fake' byelection candidates outside of Quebec
items.summary_auto: Bloc Québécois Leader Yves-François Blanchet is laughing at the idea of fielding "fake" candidates in Toronto — even if it means ceding a financial advantage to the Liberals in a tight Montreal-area byelection.

items.title: Alberta separation talk already affecting more than a quarter of surveyed Calgary businesses
items.summary_auto: Ongoing discourse around the prospect of Alberta separation is emerging as a top concern for Calgary businesses, many of whom say the debate is already impacting the local economy, a new Calgary Chamber of Commerce survey suggests.

items.title: 2nd man in a week charged with uttering threats against Ontario premier: police
items.summary_auto: A 25-year-old Hamilton man has been charged following a threat made against Ontario Premier Doug Ford, provincial police said Monday.

items.title: 2 men accused of bringing explosives to weekend protest in New York inspired by ISIS: court complaint
items.summary_auto: Two men who brought explosives to a protest outside New York City's mayoral mansion said they were inspired by the Islamic State extremist group, a court complaint said.

items.title: Not just oil: Fertilizer prices spike amid Iran war, worrying Canadian farmers
items.summary_auto: A prolonged global supply disruption could send food prices higher later this spring: analysts

items.title: IN PHOTOS | Satellite images of damage in Iran, Gulf nations
items.summary_auto: Satellite images show the damage to naval bases, airports and other key facilities inside Iran since the U.S.-Israel attacks began Feb. 28, as well as damage from Iran's retaliatory strikes on Persian Gulf neighbours.

items.title: Government names senior bureaucrat as new parliamentary budget officer
items.summary_auto: The Liberal government has named senior public servant Annette Ryan to serve as the new parliamentary budget officer.

items.title: Woman arrested following gunshots fired near Rihanna's L.A. home
items.summary_auto: No one was injured in the shooting. A 35-year-old woman was later taken into custody and the suspect has been booked on suspicion of attempted murder.

items.title: Video appears to contradict Trump claim that U.S. wasn't responsible for deadly Minab school blast
items.summary_auto: New footage shows what an expert investigative group says is likely an American Tomahawk missile hitting a compound in southern Iran, metres from the school where a deadly unclaimed blast killed more than 165 people at the start of the war raging in the Middle East.

items.title: N.L. junior hockey fight-fest sees nearly 600 minutes in penalties
items.summary_auto: The St. John's Junior Hockey League is promising "severe suspensions" following following "unacceptable" conduct on and off the ice during a Caps-Breakers playoff game in the Goulds on Saturday night.

items.title: How polar geopolitics are creeping into the Arctic Winter Games
items.summary_auto: Athletes might be the stars of the show, but the Arctic Winter Games can't escape politics. The games kicked off on Sunday with the flyover of a CF-18 fighter jet at the opening ceremony in Whitehorse. Military aircraft flying over sporting events is nothing new in much of the world, of course, but in the Canadian North, it's practically unheard of.

items.title: $30M settlement reached in class-action over alleged abuse at 2 Metro Vancouver Catholic schools
items.summary_auto: A proposed $30-million settlement has been reached in a class-action lawsuit brought by former students who allege they were sexually, physically and psychologically abused while attending two Catholic schools in Metro Vancouver between 1976 and 2013.

items.title: IN PHOTOS | Day 10: U.S.-Israel war with Iran
items.summary_auto: Iran marked the naming of Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei to replace his father as its supreme leader with a new barrage of missiles against Israel and Persian Gulf nations on Monday, as the Mideast war sent oil prices soaring.

items.title: Live Nation, U.S. Department of Justice reach settlement in antitrust trial
items.summary_auto: The proposed settlement, which still needs judge approval, includes a number of terms, including an agreement by Live Nation to cap ticketing service fees and allow venues to sell some tickets through competing platforms rather than Ticketmaster exclusively. A number of states expressed discontent with the settlement vowed to continue fighting the case without the federal government.

items.title: Environment Canada warns of 'potentially critical' freezing rain event for Quebec
items.summary_auto: Environment and Climate Change Canada issued a special weather statement warning of a "potentially critical" freezing rain situation that could start on Wednesday and continue into Thursday.

items.title: Glasgow fire destroys historic building, disrupts train travel
items.summary_auto: A major fire in the heart of Glasgow crippled Scottish train services Monday as firefighters worked to douse the blaze that destroyed a four-storey building near Scotland's busiest railway station.

items.title: Mother of Tumbler Ridge shooter sought help for mental health concerns, family and neighbours say
items.summary_auto: The B.C. NDP has promised a mental health counsellor in every school but gaps, particularly in rural regions, persist.

items.title: Oil prices dip during a manic day for markets amid U.S.-Israel war with Iran
items.summary_auto: Oil prices whipped from their highest price since 2022 back below $100 US per barrel during a volatile day for markets as the U.S. and Israel's war on Iran threatened production and shipping in the Middle East and strained energy supplies worldwide.

items.title: At least 42 people killed in Kenya floods
items.summary_auto: The death toll from heavy rains and flooding in Kenya's capital, Nairobi, and elsewhere has nearly doubled to 42, the government said ‌in a statement issued late on Sunday.

items.title: Crown to withdraw another 2 charges in sexual assault trial of Frank Stronach
items.summary_auto: Two more charges will be withdrawn in the sexual assault trial of Frank Stronach, meaning the Canadian businessman is now facing seven counts, down from the original 12.

items.title: Iran’s allies and foes, explained
items.summary_auto: Countries across the Middle East are being pulled into the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran. How long will their opposition to Iran hold?

items.title: Babies exposed to measles getting preventive treatment every week in Manitoba
items.summary_auto: An average of one baby a week in the Southern Health region is being given a preventive treatment after a potential measles exposure, which a health official says is a sign of the disease's widespread transmission and the vulnerability of babies too young to be vaccinated.

items.title: Canadian military personnel identified on white supremacist dating site
items.summary_auto: Members of the Canadian Armed Forces are among the profiles leaked from a white-only dating website that promoted white supremacist ideology, a CBC News visual investigation found.

items.title: New tuition scam left woman with $37K in fraudulent charges — and no help from bank, police or school
items.summary_auto: A new scam is emerging across Canada: fraudsters who claim to give students a tuition break, but rip them off using stolen credit cards. When a Vancouver woman discovered $37,000 in fraudulent charges on her accounts, neither the university involved, police, nor TD Bank said they could help.

items.title: Ford defends health-care record as hospitals say financial 'crisis' is coming
items.summary_auto: Premier Doug Ford is defending his government’s health-care record ahead of his eighth budget, even as the province’s hospitals say they face a billion-dollar structural funding deficit.
